title: Hold out the golden set and prevent leakage
description: Evaluation data is strictly separated from anything the system saw in training, prompts, or few-shot examples.
body: Use when building or auditing an evaluation dataset. Do not draw eval cases from the same source as few-shot examples or fine-tuning data, and avoid letting eval inputs appear in the prompt or system context. Actions: partition data so the golden set is disjoint from training/prompt material, check for near-duplicates across the boundary, and freeze the set so it is not edited to match outputs. Validation signals include a leakage check that finds no eval case overlapping training or prompt content. Risk boundary: a leaked golden set produces inflated scores that look like quality; treat detected leakage as invalidating the result and escalate before trusting it.
